title: Acourtia glomeriflora
intro: Acourtia glomeriflora, also known as clustered false goldeneye, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Acourtia glomeriflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Acourtia glomeriflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Acourtia and is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Acourtia glomeriflora is further classified as a member of the tribe Heliantheae. Within this tribe, it is closely related to other species such as Acourtia wrightii and Acourtia nana. The classification of Acourtia glomeriflora is a part of the broader taxonomic system for flowering plants.
